title: UI Agent Verification Checklist
concept: Checking: browser sandboxed, ARIA trees clean, wait states configured, safety blocks active.

exercises.prompt: What is a key part of analyzing how a web agent interacts with a website?

options.text: Checking if the website has proper safety measures
Counting how many times the agent clicked

exercises.explain: A key part of analyzing how a web agent interacts with a website is checking if the website has proper safety measures in place.

cards.title: UI Agent Verification Checklist
cards.body: To ensure safe and efficient use of web agents, we need a checklist! This checklist helps us verify that necessary safety features are in place.

cards.title: What is a UI Agent Verification Checklist?
cards.body: It's a list of steps to check if a web agent's environment is safe and organized. It helps find issues before they affect users.

cards.title: Like a Chef's Recipe
cards.body: Just like a chef checks all ingredients before cooking, we check safety and settings for web agents to ensure everything runs smoothly.

cards.title: Example Checklist Items
cards.body: 1. Browser is sandboxed. 2. ARIA trees are clean. 3. Wait states are configured. 4. Safety blocks are active.

cards.title: Myth: All Websites are Safe
cards.body: Not all websites are safe by default. Checking the verification checklist ensures a safer environment for agents.

cards.title: Safety Measures Visualized
cards.body: Visualize the checklist to see each measure for a safe web agent environment.

exercises.prompt: Why is it important to check if the browser is sandboxed?

options.text: To ensure the agent can access all websites
To prevent harmful actions from affecting the computer
To make the web page load faster
To allow the agent to save files directly

exercises.explain: Checking if the browser is sandboxed is important to prevent harmful actions from affecting the computer, keeping it safe.

exercises.prompt: True or False: ARIA trees should be messy and unorganized for better usability.
exercises.explain: False! ARIA trees should be clean and organized to ensure better usability for all users.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence: Waiting states need to be __________ so the agent doesn’t act too quickly.
exercises.sentence: Waiting states need to be configured so the agent doesn’t act too quickly.
exercises.explain: Waiting states need to be configured so the agent doesn’t act too quickly. This helps the agent work efficiently.

exercises.prompt: Which option represents a proper verification step for a UI agent?
exercises.explain: Ensuring safety blocks are active is a proper verification step for a UI agent, protecting the environment.

exercises.prompt: Which pair of configurations is essential for a safe UI agent environment?

options.text: Clean ARIA trees & active safety blocks
Active safety blocks & multiple browsers open

exercises.explain: Clean ARIA trees and active safety blocks are essential for a safe UI agent environment, ensuring users’ interactions are manageable and secure.
